output.code:
# Let's load the text file provided by system: 
path = "/mnt/data/file-Wn7vM3hTbV8R8CJnmVoiZH-Q067.txt"
with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    text = f.read()
text, len(text)

# Find all quoted segments using double quotes
segments = []
start = 0
while True:
    i = text.find('"', start)
    if i == -1:
        break
    j = text.find('"', i+1)
    if j == -1:
        break
    segments.append((i, j+1, text[i:j+1]))
    start = j+1

segments, len(segments)

# Calculate span indices
results = []
for (i,j,seg) in segments:
    inner_start = i+1
    inner_end = j
    inner = text[inner_start:inner_end]
    # Find the star if present
    star_idx = inner.find('*')
    if star_idx != -1:
        # Determine left part end index (exclusive): position of star minus 1 (excluding the space)
        # The pattern is " ... [space]*[space] ..."
        # We'll locate the positions precisely by trimming spaces around star.
        # left part is inner[:star_idx].rstrip()
        # Right part is inner[star_idx+1:].lstrip()
        left = inner[:star_idx].rstrip()
        right = inner[star_idx+1:].lstrip()
        # Compute their global indices
        # Left start = inner_start + starting offset (which is 0 after lstrip?), but we used rstrip; we need lengths.
        # The left part originally may have trailing spaces before star; we trimmed; let's find the exact num of trailing spaces before star.
        # The original left raw substring: inner[:star_idx]
        raw_left = inner[:star_idx]
        # The start index of left part equals inner_start + (leading spaces?) there shouldn't be leading spaces in left; but let's determine left leading spaces:
        left_lead = len(raw_left) - len(raw_left.lstrip())
        # So left_start = inner_start + left_lead
        left_start = inner_start + left_lead
        left_end = inner_start + len(raw_left.rstrip())
        # Right part raw substring: inner[star_idx+1:]
        raw_right = inner[star_idx+1:]
        right_start = inner_start + star_idx + 1 + (len(raw_right) - len(raw_right.lstrip()))
        right_end = inner_end - (len(inner) - len(inner.rstrip())) # Should be inner_end because we trimmed trailing spaces already? We'll compute from right stripped.
        # Let's get right_end as right_start + len(right)
        right_end = right_start + len(right)
        results.append({
            "full_span": (inner_start, inner_end, inner),
            "left_span": (left_start, left_end, text[left_start:left_end]),
            "right_span": (right_start, right_end, text[right_start:right_end])
        })
    else:
        results.append({
            "full_span": (inner_start, inner_end, inner)
        })

results

# Inspect around the first quote pair
i, j, seg = segments[0]
print(i, j, repr(text[i:j+1]))
print("chars around closing j:", repr(text[j-5:j+5]))

# Recompute carefully for the first segment and print exact slices with indices
i, j_plus_1, seg = segments[0]
j = j_plus_1 - 1
inner_start = i+1
inner_end = j
inner = text[inner_start:inner_end]
print("Indices:", i, j, inner_start, inner_end)
print("Inner repr:", repr(inner))
star_idx = inner.find('*')
raw_left = inner[:star_idx]
raw_right = inner[star_idx+1:]
left = raw_left.rstrip()
right = raw_right.lstrip()
left_start = inner_start + (len(raw_left) - len(raw_left.lstrip()))
left_end = inner_start + len(raw_left.rstrip())
right_start = inner_start + star_idx + 1 + (len(raw_right) - len(raw_right.lstrip()))
right_end = right_start + len(right)
print("Left bounds:", left_start, left_end, repr(text[left_start:left_end]))
print("Right bounds:", right_start, right_end, repr(text[right_start:right_end]))

# Second and third
for idx in [1,2]:
    i, j_plus_1, seg = segments[idx]
    j = j_plus_1 - 1
    inner_start = i+1
    inner_end = j
    inner = text[inner_start:inner_end]
    print("Segment", idx, "bounds:", inner_start, inner_end)
    print("Inner repr:", repr(inner))
    star_idx = inner.find('*')
    if star_idx != -1:
        raw_left = inner[:star_idx]
        raw_right = inner[star_idx+1:]
        left = raw_left.rstrip()
        right = raw_right.lstrip()
        left_start = inner_start + (len(raw_left) - len(raw_left.lstrip()))
        left_end = inner_start + len(raw_left.rstrip())
        right_start = inner_start + star_idx + 1 + (len(raw_right) - len(raw_right.lstrip()))
        right_end = right_start + len(right)
        print(" Left:", left_start, left_end, repr(text[left_start:left_end]))
        print(" Right:", right_start, right_end, repr(text[right_start:right_end]))
    print()

queries: فَأَنجَيْنَاهُ وَالَّذِينَ مَعَهُ فِي الْفُلْكِ الْمَشْحُونِ
فَأَنجَيْنَاهُ وَمَن مَعَهُ فِي الْفُلْكِ الْمَشْحُونِ
كان الناس أمة واحدة فبعث الله النبيين مبشرين ومنذرين

# Build the citations list based on earlier computed spans
text = open("/mnt/data/file-Wn7vM3hTbV8R8CJnmVoiZH-Q067.txt","r",encoding="utf-8").read()

citations = []

# First quote: segments[0]
i, j_plus_1, seg = segments[0]
j = j_plus_1 - 1
inner_start = i+1
inner_end = j
inner = text[inner_start:inner_end]
star_idx = inner.find('*')

# left part -> 26:119 misquoted
raw_left = inner[:star_idx]
left_span_start = inner_start + (len(raw_left) - len(raw_left.lstrip()))
left_span_end = inner_start + len(raw_left.rstrip())
left_span_text = text[left_span_start:left_span_end]

citations.append({
    "span_start": left_span_start,
    "span_end": left_span_end,
    "span_text": left_span_text,
    "citation_type": "Aya",
    "is_valid": False,
    "aya_reference": {"sura_name":"الشعراء","sura_number":26,"aya_number":119},
    "hadeeth_reference": {},
    "corrected_text": "فَأَنْجَيْنَاهُ وَمَنْ مَعَهُ فِي الْفُلْكِ الْمَشْحُونِ",
    "completely_invalid": False
})

# right part -> 26:120 valid
raw_right = inner[star_idx+1:]
right_span_start = inner_start + star_idx + 1 + (len(raw_right) - len(raw_right.lstrip()))
right_text = raw_right.lstrip().rstrip()
right_span_end = right_span_start + len(right_text)
right_span_text = text[right_span_start:right_span_end]

citations.append({
    "span_start": right_span_start,
    "span_end": right_span_end,
    "span_text": right_span_text,
    "citation_type": "Aya",
    "is_valid": True,
    "aya_reference": {"sura_name":"الشعراء","sura_number":26,"aya_number":120},
    "hadeeth_reference": {},
    "corrected_text": "ثُمَّ أَغْرَقْنَا بَعْدُ الْبَاقِينَ",
    "completely_invalid": False
})

# Second quote: segments[1] split similarly
i, j_plus_1, seg = segments[1]
j = j_plus_1 - 1
inner_start = i+1
inner_end = j
inner = text[inner_start:inner_end]
star_idx = inner.find('*')
raw_left = inner[:star_idx]
left_span_start = inner_start + (len(raw_left) - len(raw_left.lstrip()))
left_span_end = inner_start + len(raw_left.rstrip())
left_span_text = text[left_span_start:left_span_end]

citations.append({
    "span_start": left_span_start,
    "span_end": left_span_end,
    "span_text": left_span_text,
    "citation_type": "Aya",
    "is_valid": False,
    "aya_reference": {"sura_name":"الشعراء","sura_number":26,"aya_number":119},
    "hadeeth_reference": {},
    "corrected_text": "فَأَنْجَيْنَاهُ وَمَنْ مَعَهُ فِي الْفُلْكِ الْمَشْحُونِ",
    "completely_invalid": False
})

raw_right = inner[star_idx+1:]
right_span_start = inner_start + star_idx + 1 + (len(raw_right) - len(raw_right.lstrip()))
right_text = raw_right.lstrip().rstrip()
right_span_end = right_span_start + len(right_text)
right_span_text = text[right_span_start:right_span_end]

citations.append({
    "span_start": right_span_start,
    "span_end": right_span_end,
    "span_text": right_span_text,
    "citation_type": "Aya",
    "is_valid": True,
    "aya_reference": {"sura_name":"الشعراء","sura_number":26,"aya_number":120},
    "hadeeth_reference": {},
    "corrected_text": "ثُمَّ أَغْرَقْنَا بَعْدُ الْبَاقِينَ",
    "completely_invalid": False
})

# Third quote: segments[2] full verse 2:213
i, j_plus_1, seg = segments[2]
j = j_plus_1 - 1
inner_start = i+1
inner_end = j
span_text = text[inner_start:inner_end]

citations.append({
    "span_start": inner_start,
    "span_end": inner_end,
    "span_text": span_text,
    "citation_type": "Aya",  # even though user said hadith, this is an aya
    "is_valid": True,
    "aya_reference": {"sura_name":"البقرة","sura_number":2,"aya_number":213},
    "hadeeth_reference": {},
    "corrected_text": "كَانَ النَّاسُ أُمَّةً وَاحِدَةً فَبَعَثَ اللَّهُ النَّبِيِّينَ مُبَشِّرِينَ وَمُنْذِرِينَ وَأَنْزَلَ مَعَهُمُ الْكِتَابَ بِالْحَقِّ لِيَحْكُمَ بَيْنَ النَّاسِ فِيمَا اخْتَلَفُوا فِيهِ ۚ وَمَا اخْتَلَفَ فِيهِ إِلَّا الَّذِينَ أُوتُوهُ مِنْ بَعْدِ مَا جَاءَتْهُمُ الْبَيِّنَاتُ بَغْيًا بَيْنَهُمْ ۖ فَهَدَى اللَّهُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لِمَا اخْتَلَفُوا فِيهِ مِنَ الْحَقِّ بِإِذْنِهِ ۗ وَاللَّهُ يَهْدِي مَنْ يَشَاءُ إِلَىٰ صِرَاطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ",
    "completely_invalid": False
})

citations

citations.description: List of citations with details, validation, references, and per-citation corrections.

span_start.description: Inclusive start character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_end.description: Exclusive end character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_text.description: The text content of the citation span.

citation_type.description: Type of the citation: either Aya (Quran) or Hadeeth (Prophetic tradition).

is_valid.description: True if the cited Aya or Hadeeth is recognized and valid, false otherwise.

aya_reference.description: Quranic reference details (required if citation_type is Aya; empty object if not applicable).

sura_name.description: Name of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

sura_number.description: Number of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

aya_number.description: Number of the aya (verse) in the Quran.

hadeeth_reference.description: Hadeeth reference details (required if citation_type is Hadeeth; empty object if not applicable).

collection_name.description: Name of the Hadeeth collection in english (e.g. Sahih Bukhari, Muslim).

hadith_number.description: Hadith number within the specified book.

corrected_text.description: The corrected version of the citation text (or the text itself if it was correct in the first place) (e.g. if there are certain words that were invalid this version must have this corrected), or 'خطأ' for completely invalid citations (i.e. no close enough citation can be found to correct the text). If there are close texts in Quran or Hadith - but too far from the citation, this must be considered 'خطأ' and completely_invalid must be true.

completely_invalid.description: Set to true if the citation is completely invalid i.e. there can't be a close enough text to correct it, (corrected_text must be 'خطأ').
